reason: The statement applies because Saul, the protagonist in this text fragment, is depicted as a king who is given a specific command by the LORD to utterly destroy the Amalekites and all their possessions. However, Saul disobeys this command by sparing the king of the Amalekites and the best of the livestock, claiming he did so to offer sacrifices to the LORD. This disobedience results in his rejection as king by the LORD, showcasing his flaws and failures despite his initial anointing as king over Israel.
